subject: Continuous Random Variables
question: The waiting time X (in minutes) of a train arrival to a station has an exponential distribution with mean 3 minutes. (a) What is the probability of having to wait 6 or more minutes for a train? (b) What is the probability of waiting between 4 and 7 minutes for a train? (c) What is the probability of having to wait at least 9 more minutes for the train given that you have already waited 3 minutes?
